plan: Sell all SOXL, CRWD, and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs and flagged holdings.
Trim SMH to reduce semiconductor concentration.
Increase SPY and QQQI to favor broad index and income.
Maintain a strong cash buffer for capital preservation.
Focus on low-drawdown, high-quality names.
Keep single name and sector concentrations within limits.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation, targeting around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces risk by exiting leveraged positions and high-volatility names. Expect lower potential returns but also much smaller drawdowns.

plan: Sell CRWD to exit the flagged position.
Trim SOXL and SPAL to keep leveraged exposure small.
Reduce SMH to lower semiconductor concentration.
Increase SPY and QQQ for a stronger broad index core.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer around 10%.
Blend broad index, quality tech, and small leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan focuses on risk-first growth, aiming for approximately 2.5% per month. It trims leverage and concentration while building a solid core of broad index funds. Expect moderate returns with manageable drawdowns.

plan: Buy CRWD based on the scanner's high-R setup.
Increase SOXL and SPAL to lean into leveraged ETFs.
Add TQQQ as a new leveraged ETF position.
Trim SMH and SPY to fund higher-conviction, higher-beta plays.
Concentrate in high-scoring scanner setups for aggressive growth.
Maintain a small cash buffer, using wider but strict stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets stretch growth, aiming for around 4% per month. It significantly increases exposure to leveraged ETFs and concentrated high-beta scanner setups. Be aware that this mode carries substantially higher drawdown risk and leveraged ETFs experience daily decay, requiring strict stop discipline to manage potential rapid losses.

plan: Sell all SOXL, CRWD, SPAL, and SMH to eliminate leverage and high-beta exposure.
Maximize QQQI for primary income generation.
Increase SPY significantly for broad market exposure and stability.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable monthly distributions.
Keep a cash buffer for liquidity.
Rotate into income/covered-call ETFs.
income.read: This income plan aims to park cash for yield, targeting around 1% per month. It focuses on maximizing income and broad index exposure while cutting all leveraged and high-beta positions. While prioritizing low drawdown and distributions, remember that distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a market selloff.
